step_by_step_reasoning: The OutSystems monolith application data is decomposed into four distinct AWS purpose-built storage services based on data type. S3 holds binary/blob data; Aurora Serverless stores tenant relational databases; DynamoDB holds configuration and key-value pairs; Secrets Manager directly secures sensitive encrypted data. KMS manages customer keys (BYOK) to encrypt the tenant data in S3 and Aurora.

graph.name: OutSystems Monolith Data Decomposition
graph.notes: Decomposes a monolith platform's data into specialized AWS storage services to achieve multi-tenancy, scale, and cost-efficiency while supporting tenant-specific encryption.

nodes.name: Monolith Data Types
nodes.notes: The original monolithic application data split into Binary, Relational, Key-Value, and Encrypted segments.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: Stores unstructured binary blobs and key-value document data.

nodes.name: Aurora Serverless
nodes.notes: Provides independent database instances per tenant for core relational data.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Offloads configuration and key-value data to improve agility and reduce costs.

nodes.name: Secrets Manager
nodes.notes: Directly stores sensitive encrypted data, offloading security from relational DBs.

nodes.name: KMS
nodes.notes: Manages tenant-specific keys (BYOK) used to encrypt S3 and Aurora resources.

edges.notes: Relational data routed to Aurora Serverless
Encrypted data routed to Secrets Manager
Tenant-specific KMS key encrypts S3 bucket
Tenant-specific KMS key encrypts Aurora instance
